title: Arctic Ice Shrinks in Winter as Earth Gets Warmer
summary: Arctic sea ice shrank to tie its lowest winter level ever measured. This is surprising because winter is when ice usually grows. The Earth is getting warmer, breaking heat records in many places like the United States, Mexico, and Australia. Arctic ice is important because it reflects sunlight, helping to keep the planet cool. Without enough ice, more heat goes into the oceans. This affects animals like polar bears and seals that need ice to live. Scientists say the ice is shrinking steadily due to warming temperatures. Less ice in winter means a head start for summer melting, which can lead to more extreme weather. The ice area was much smaller than the average from 1981 to 2010, about twice the size of Texas. Melting sea ice does not make sea levels rise, but it changes the climate.

questions.question: Does melting sea ice make sea levels rise?

background_read: The Arctic is a cold region at the top of the Earth covered with ice. Sea ice forms on the ocean and helps keep our planet cool by reflecting sunlight. In winter, it usually grows, but warming from climate change is causing it to shrink. This affects wildlife and weather patterns globally. Understanding this helps us see how small changes in one place can impact the whole world.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists like Walt Meier and organizations like the National Snow and Ice Data Center
WHAT: Arctic sea ice shrank to tie its lowest winter level, and Earth broke heat records
WHERE: Arctic region, United States, Mexico, Australia, and other parts of the world
WHY: Because the Earth is warming, leading to less ice growth in winter and more extreme weather

perspectives.description: The shrinking ice shows climate change is harming the Arctic and could lead to more problems like extreme weather.
